events.label: reasoning
events.text:
The scenario describes a missing box extension on a deep finish in an electrical installation at rough-complete stage. This means a box was installed but when the wall finish (like drywall/plaster) was applied, the box ended up recessed too far back because an extension/raised cover wasn't installed. This is a common code issue.

NEC 314.20 requires that boxes be installed so they will be set back not more than 1/4 inch in noncombustible surfaces. If the finish is deep, the box needs an extension to bring it flush. Missing box extension = non-compliant.

This is a fail. The box is recessed beyond tolerance (likely more than 1/4 inch), which is a defect.

    "Conductors entering the box may not meet the 6 in. free length / 3 in. outside box requirement of NEC 300.14 if the recess is significant."
  ],

    "Install an approved box extension or raised cover to bring the box face flush with or within 1/4 in. of the finished surface.",
    "Verify conductor free length at each affected box after extension is added; re-dress and re-identify as needed."
  ],
  "rationale": "A missing box extension on a deep finish leaves the box face recessed beyond the NEC-permitted 1/4 in. setback for noncombustible wall surfaces, making the installation non-compliant and potentially affecting conductor termination length and device mounting.",
